skill.name: grilling
skill.description: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design. Use when the user wants to stress-test a plan before building, or uses any 'grill' trigger phrases.
skill.summary: Relentlessly interview the user about a plan or design to surface hidden assumptions before building.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 3 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:6. After manual review, all 3 are FALSE POSITIVES — the file contains only plain markdown text (a skill description and instructions for an interview workflow) with no executable code, no cryptographic operations, and no security-relevant patterns. The file is 11 lines of natural language instructions with no scripts, network calls, filesystem access, or external commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged lines 3 and 6 as containing weak cryptographic algorithm references. Manual review confirms these are false positives — the lines contain only natural language markdown text describing the skill's purpose (interviewing the user about a plan). No cryptographic code or patterns exist in the file.

content.user_title: Stress-test any plan before you build it
content.value_statement: Plans often fail because hidden assumptions go unchallenged. This skill makes the AI relentlessly interview you about every aspect of your design, surfacing gaps and resolving dependencies one question at a time.
actual_capabilities: Interviews the user one question at a time about every aspect of a plan or design
Walks down each branch of the design tree, resolving dependencies between decisions
Explores the codebase to answer questions when possible instead of asking the user
Provides a recommended answer for each question to guide the discussion
Waits for user feedback on each question before continuing to the next
limitations: Asks one question at a time, which may feel slow for simple plans
Does not make decisions for the user — it surfaces questions and provides recommendations only
Codebase exploration is limited to what the AI can access in the current session
Designed for planning and design review, not for execution or implementation tasks

use_cases.title: Review an architecture decision
use_cases.description: Stress-test a proposed system architecture before writing code by having the AI challenge every assumption and dependency.

use_cases.title: Validate a product roadmap
use_cases.description: Walk through a product plan branch by branch, resolving sequencing and dependency questions one at a time.

use_cases.title: Pressure-test a side project idea
use_cases.description: Use the grilling workflow to find weak spots in a hobby or side project plan before investing time building it.

prompt_templates.title: Basic plan review
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill me on my plan to add a user notification system to my app.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start — user wants to stress-test a simple feature plan

prompt_templates.title: Architecture stress test
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm designing a microservices architecture for a logistics platform. Grill me on every aspect until we reach a shared understanding.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate — user wants a thorough review of a complex system design

prompt_templates.title: Dependency-focused review
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill me about my migration plan from PostgreSQL to MongoDB, focusing on how each decision depends on the others.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced — user wants to surface hidden dependencies between design choices

prompt_templates.title: Codebase-informed interview
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill me about my plan to refactor the authentication module. Explore the codebase first before asking questions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced — user wants the AI to leverage codebase context during the interview

output_examples.input: Grill me on my plan to add dark mode to my React app.
output_examples.output:
What styling approach are you considering — CSS variables, a theme provider library, or something else? I recommend CSS variables with a ThemeProvider context because it keeps the toggle logic minimal and works well with SSR.

[waits for user response before continuing]

output_examples.input: I'm planning to switch from REST to GraphQL. Grill me.
output_examples.output:
What's the primary motivation — reducing over-fetching, simplifying the client, or something else? I'd recommend starting with over-fetching as the anchor because it makes the strongest cost case for the migration.

[waits for user response before continuing]

best_practices: Start with a clear, specific plan statement so the AI knows what to grill you about
Answer each question honestly — the value comes from surfacing weak assumptions, not defending them
Use the recommended answers as a starting point, but make your own final decision
Allow codebase exploration when relevant — it reduces the number of questions you need to answer manually
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for tasks that require immediate execution or code generation
Do not expect multiple questions at once — the skill is intentionally one-question-at-a-time
Do not skip providing a recommended answer when asking the AI to continue, as it loses the coaching value

faq.question: What triggers the grilling workflow?
faq.answer: Any message containing the word 'grill' or phrases like 'stress-test my plan', 'interview me about', or 'challenge my design' will activate this skill.

faq.question: How many questions will the AI ask?
faq.answer: The AI asks one question at a time and waits for your response before continuing. The total number depends on the complexity of your plan.

faq.question: Can the AI explore my codebase instead of asking?
faq.answer: Yes. If a question can be answered by exploring the codebase, the AI will explore the codebase instead of asking you.

faq.question: Does the AI make decisions for me?
faq.answer: No. The AI provides a recommended answer for each question, but the final decision is always yours.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill contains only natural language instructions. It does not execute code, access the network, modify files, or run external commands.

faq.question: What tools does this skill work with?
faq.answer: This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
